mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 7.5% indicates tightening labor market conditions, which historically correlate with stronger corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines below 7.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI inflation and declines in the federal funds rate create a 'Goldilocks' environment where central banks maintain accommodative policy while inflation remains manageable, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% YoY and FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction permits (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signal strong demand-side momentum, which historically predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS exceed their respective thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) indicate weakening supply-side activity, which historically correlates with negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days due to reduced corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of periods with declining INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly gain in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 150k signals robust labor market health, which historically predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP growth (GDPC1) and rising housing starts (HOUST) create conflicting demand signals, which historically predict negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days during recessionary transitions.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 50%+ of periods where GDPC1 declines below 2% YoY and HOUST exceeds 650k.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 1% signals persistent inflationary pressure with accommodative policy, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS is below 1%.
